title: Kinabaluchloa wrayi
intro: Kinabaluchloa wrayi is a species of flowering plant in the family Poaceae, commonly known as a type of grass. Traditionally, Kinabaluchloa wrayi has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its reported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Kinabaluchloa wrayi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are thought to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Kinabaluchloa wrayi is often made into tea, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional remedies for its purported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.
